allow: PowerShell(Start-Sleep -Seconds 1; $conn = Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 4100 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if \($conn\) { Stop-Process -Id $conn.OwningProcess -Force; Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500 }; if \(Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 4100 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue\) { "still up" } else { "port free" })

PowerShell(Start-Sleep -Seconds 2; $conn = Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 4100 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if \($conn\) { try { Stop-Process -Id $conn.OwningProcess -Force -ErrorAction Stop } catch {} ; Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 800 }; if \(Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 4100 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue\) { "still up" } else { "port free" })

PowerShell(Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 4100 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object LocalPort, State, OwningProcess | Format-List; Write-Output "---procs---"; Get-Process -Id \(Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 4100 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue\).OwningProcess -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Id, ProcessName | Format-List)

PowerShell($ports = @\(3000, 3200, 4100, 4040\); foreach \($p in $ports\) { $c = Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort $p -State Listen -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 1; if \($c\) { "Port $p : UP" } else { "Port $p : DOWN" } })
